name: Process Scheduling
description: The CPU scheduler decides which ready process runs next: processes enter the ready queue and a policy such as round-robin or priority/multilevel feedback selects one to dispatch; a running process is preempted on quantum expiry or when it blocks, returning to the queue, until it finishes and terminates.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Processes ready"]
    N2["Add to ready queue"]
    N3{"Select policy"}
    N4["Round-robin slice"]
    N5["Priority / MLFQ"]
    N6["Dispatch to CPU"]
    N7["Process runs"]
    N8{"Quantum expired/block?"}
    N9["Preempt + switch"]
    N10{"Process finished?"}
    N11["Terminate process"]
    N12["Fair CPU allocation"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|round-robin| N4
    N3 -->|priority| N5
    N4 --> N6
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 -->|requeue| N2
    N8 -->|no| N10
    N10 -->|no| N7
    N10 -->|yes| N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
